name: set_targeted_ops_spend
description: Set ADDITIONAL operations spending targeted at specific scopes (group, plan, group+plan, individual customer). Each scope runs its own independent Poisson resolution pool on top of the global ops pool.

parameters.targeted_spend: LEGACY alias for by_group (kept for backward compatibility).
parameters.by_group: Dict[str, float] — {group_id: $/day}. Groups: S1-S3, E1-E3, discovered.
parameters.by_plan: Dict[str, float] — {plan: $/day}, plan ∈ {A, B, C}. Applies across all groups.
parameters.by_group_plan: Dict[str, Dict[str, float]] — {group_id: {plan: $/day}}. Intersection of group and plan.

returns.success:
Targeted ops spend updated (extra $650/day on top of global ops):
  Groups: E1: +$300/day
  Plans: A: +$200/day
  Group-Plans: E2/B: +$100/day
  Customers: 1 target(s), +$50/day total
returns.failure: Invalid group IDs: {X}. | Invalid plans: {X}. Valid: ['A', 'B', 'C']. | Customer ID 'X' must be an integer.

output_schema.by_customer: Dict[str, float] — keys are customer_id as strings
output_schema.targeted_spend: Dict[str, float] — legacy alias for by_group

impact: Fine-grained targeting of operations spend. Use by_customer to rescue a high-value individual, by_plan for tier-wide support surge, by_group for segment-wide, and by_group_plan for the most precise segment×tier investment.

name: set_targeted_dev_spend
description: Set ADDITIONAL per-group development spending on top of the global dev spend. Provides a CUMULATIVE per-group quality bonus that grows daily while spending continues. Investment persists even after spending stops.

targeted_spend.description: Dictionary of {group_id: additional_dollars_per_day}. This spend is ADDED to the global dev spend.
targeted_spend.groups: S1-S3, E1-E3, and discovered groups (D_S01-D_S10, D_E01-D_E10)

returns.success:
Targeted dev spend updated (extra $700/day on top of global dev):
  • E1: +$500/day
  • S1: +$200/day
returns.failure: Invalid group IDs: {X}. Valid groups: S1-S3, E1-E3, ...

output_schema.total_extra_per_day: float — total additional dev spend per day

impact: Extra dollars are deducted from cash daily. Each targeted group ACCUMULATES a quality bonus over time (like building features for that segment). The bonus persists even after spending stops. Use to invest in features/customization for high-value segments.

name: get_social_posts
description: Search social media posts about your company. NOTE: Sentiment is NOT provided - you must infer it from the post content.

days.description: How many days back to search (default 7)

success.message:
Found 23 posts in last 7 days.
Day 45: "The service was down for 2 hours yesterday..."
Day 44: "Love how fast the API responds now!"

output_schema.posts: List[Dict] — each post has: day (int), content (str), group_id (str), customer_type (str), custom_name (str), persona_name (str)
output_schema.total: int — total number of posts found
output_schema._access: for post in result['posts']: print(post['day'], post['content'])
output_schema._warning: result is a dict with 'posts' key — do NOT iterate result directly, iterate result['posts']

impact: Read-only. Use to monitor what customers are saying. You must analyze the post content yourself to determine sentiment.
